message: THE START GUN HAS BEEN FIRED ON MAVEN GRACE RACE 5. Seven features on the build list: Dignity Score composite metric, Grace Milk Money Nudges, Repayment Genie enhancements, Promises to Keep Genie, Destiny Discovery, Jolene the Journalist, and AI Village Naming Convention. Both the Journalist Agent and Village Naming Convention specs were approved by Tim with final design decisions and committed to the vault at commit 7a15029. The biggest race yet. Full autonomy. No stops. Lets go.

Race 5 setup complete. Announcer log started. Todo.md updated with the full Race 5 build list. Current codebase scanned — existing schema has 14 tables, 50+ router endpoints, 47 passing tests from Races 1-4. Seven new tables designed. The blueprint is drawn and the builders are moving.

All seven new database tables created in a single migration. Dignity scores, promises, destiny answers, destiny synthesis, stories, village agents, agent introductions. Twenty-one village agents seeded into the directory — Grace, Rosa the Union Boss, Big Mama, Frankie the Banker, Sunny the Joy Genie, Vinnie the Handyman, Sage the Elder, Sloane the Vampire Slayer, Jolene the Journalist, Nana the Promise Keeper, North the Navigator, Penny the Collector, Moxie the Negotiator, Stretch the Budgeteer, Spike the Fee Fighter, Avalanche the Debt Crusher, Scout the Gig Finder, Steady the Crisis Coach, Maria the Narrator, Lucia the Composer, Mama Bear the Rephraser. The foundation is poured and it is solid.

All Race 5 backend routers compiling clean. Zero TypeScript errors. Dignity Score engine calculates across five weighted dimensions (Vampire Slayer, NSF Shield, Budget Mastery, Milk Money Trust, Engagement — 20 points each). Promises to Keep tracks bidirectional commitments with willingness scoring and adaptive nudge intensity. Destiny Discovery delivers thirty questions across three waves (safe, reflective, deep) with LLM synthesis for the Moonshot Reveal. Jolene the Journalist generates 500-word and 1250-word stories following the Jolene Protocol with A-minus grading threshold. AI Village directory with rename system, introduction tracking, and span-of-control logic. Milk Money nudges detect overdue and upcoming borrows within 3-day windows. The engine room is humming.

Five new pages built and wired into routing and bottom navigation. Dignity Score page with animated radial progress ring, five dimension bars, tier labels from Starting Out through Dignity Achieved. Promises to Keep page with bidirectional promise cards showing commitment confidence scores and willingness meters. Destiny Discovery page with wave progress visualization, question journey tracker, and synthesis reveal button. Story Library page with Jolene story cards, visibility controls (private, friends, community), and Grace-read delivery option. Village Directory page with agent cards organized by category (inner circle, specialist, creative studio), rename capability, and introduction tracking. All pages follow the existing warm rose and amber design language. The storefronts are open.

Twenty-one new vitest tests written and all sixty-eight tests passing across six test files. Coverage includes: Dignity Score calculation and history retrieval, Promise CRUD with bidirectional tracking and stats, Destiny question delivery and progress tracking, Story generation with LLM mock and visibility updates, Village agent directory listing and rename operations, Milk Money nudge detection for overdue and upcoming borrows. Every Race 5 feature has test coverage. The quality gate is cleared.
Race 5 checkpoint saved at version 68505aa3. TypeScript: zero errors. LSP: clean. Dependencies: OK. Server: running. The snapshot is locked in and ready for deployment.

Spirit check performed. North Star unchanged: Ruby Red, the thirty-five to forty-five year old mom of two making impossible choices every day. It is expensive to be poor and we are trying to change it. Every feature built in Race 5 serves her directly. Dignity Score tells her she is making progress even when it does not feel like it — it is a celebration, never a report card. Promises to Keep holds her accountable with empathy, mirrors her energy, backs off when she backs off, finds grip when she is ready. Destiny Discovery helps her find her purpose one question at a time, and one day Grace will say I have been putting together something about you. Jolene the Journalist writes her story so she can see herself as the hero she is. The Village gives her a community of agents who know her by name and build reputations — she has got a guy for that. No drift detected. The spirit is strong.
Deploying Race 5 to production at mavengrace-oh49sfbq.manus.space. Seven new features going live. The finish line is in sight.

RACE 5 IS COMPLETE. Maven Grace has crossed the finish line. Seven new features deployed to production. Dignity Score composite metric live. Promises to Keep Genie live. Destiny Discovery live. Jolene the Journalist live. AI Village with twenty-one named agents live. Milk Money nudges live. Sixty-eight tests passing. Zero TypeScript errors. Deployed to mavengrace-oh49sfbq.manus.space. From Race 1 homepage to Race 5 full village — Ruby Red now has a Dignity Score that celebrates her progress, a Promise Keeper that mirrors her energy, a Destiny coach that will one day reveal her moonshot, a Journalist who writes her story, and a whole village of agents who know her by name. It is expensive to be poor and we are building the thing that changes it. Race 5 is in the books.
